danger_signals.visual_form: Giant spiraling vortex pulling upward from water surface
danger_signals.meaning: Catastrophic threat; harbinger of alien harvest cycle; separation from pod/safety

danger_signals.visual_form: Decreasing air gauge UI element above health meter
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent drowning danger; time pressure; mammalian vulnerability

danger_signals.visual_form: Hostile marine creatures (sharks, octopods, seahorses) with attack animations
danger_signals.meaning: Direct physical threat; environmental hazard; health loss

danger_signals.visual_form: Turbulent water, reduced visibility, chaotic environmental behavior
danger_signals.meaning: World-state disruption; cyclical catastrophe; alien interference
Environmental obstacle; puzzle gate requiring key-glyph solution

safety_signals.visual_form: Friendly dolphins swimming in formation near player
safety_signals.meaning: Social safety; community; home state before catastrophe

safety_signals.visual_form: Bubbling water columns or designated breathing zones
safety_signals.meaning: Respiration opportunity; survival resource; safe pause point
Health/air recovery when sung to; cooperative marine life

safety_signals.visual_form: Whales, dolphins, other marine mammals with non-hostile behavior
Special crystalline formations with healing aura
safety_signals.meaning: Health and invulnerability restoration; ancient Atlantean aid

safety_signals.visual_form: Massive blue whale in Arctic region
safety_signals.meaning: Wisdom source; quest anchor; protective elder authority

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Dolphin and whale silhouettes; song/sonar waves
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Ocean bays, Arctic waters, coral regions; natural marine habitats
Sunken ruins; Atlantis city; ancient libraries; time machine technology; pyramid-like structures

faction_indicators.color_identity: Unnatural purples, sickly greens, cosmic blacks
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Waterspout vortex form; tube-like spacecraft; alien Queen
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Vortex creatures appearing as aberrant sea life; mechanized hostile entities; Queen as boss entity
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Waterspout phenomena; outer space void; alien spacecraft interior (geometric, non-organic architecture); planetary Vortex world

faction_indicators.color_identity: Ancient stone/mineral coloration; luminous when powered
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Asterite appears as temporary hostile until Ecco retrieves its orb; transforms to ally after gift exchange
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Deep-sea cavern; prehistoric past (55 million years); primordial ocean floor

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Calm bay waters; peaceful pod swimming; clear visibility; stable weather
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Pod members engage in play (jump challenge); Ecco can move freely; no enemy pressure

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Giant waterspout forms; water becomes turbulent; visibility reduces; sky darkens
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Pod separated/vanishes; Ecco alone; chaos spreads across ocean regions; marine life scattered and fearful

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Turbulent waters throughout accessible regions; weather patterns chaotic; increased visual noise and distortion
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Marine life hostile or fleeing; navigation difficult; increased enemy encounters; NPCs report coast-to-coast disruption

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Prehistoric landscape; different ocean color palette; Pteranodon visible; primordial atmosphere; archaic Asterite form
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Time-displaced creatures; ancient Asterite more aggressive; different enemy types; temporal instability visual effects

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Non-organic geometry; artificial lighting; cosmic void backgrounds; unnatural material textures; spacecraft interior corridors
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Vortex creatures continuously hostile; no organic life; mechanical hazards; Queen entity as final conflict point

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Waterspout dissipates; waters return to calm; lighting normalizes; sky clears
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Threat neutralized; pod could potentially reconvene; world returns to equilibrium; NPC behavior stabilizes

environmental_storytelling.example: Other dolphins report storm felt ocean-wide; Big Blue explains 500-year cycle; Asterite reveals Vortex nature through exposition
Sunken Atlantis ruins contain library of Glyphs encoding alien-war history; architecture shows battle damage and decay
Pod disappearance into waterspout; storm chaos spreading from region to region indicates escalating threat scope
Fish and clams abundant in safe zones; sparse in hostile regions; air vents mark established safe corridors
Information-bearing Glyphs in Atlantean library provide game lore; blocking Glyphs serve puzzle progression; restorative Glyphs reward exploration
Vortex Queen as final encounter embodies alien threat; Asterite as ancient guardian embodies Earth's primordial defense

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Free swimming; song interaction with friendlies; health stable; air available
Ramming attacks on enemies; defensive maneuvers; urgent air-seeking; health loss visible
Social play; formation swimming; responsive to Ecco's presence
Vanished/separated; no longer present in world; represents lost community
Respond to song with dialogue; provide directions; offer wisdom; non-aggressive positioning
Attack on proximity; pursuit patterns; visual aggression displays
Attacks Ecco on first encounter; regains orbs restore agency
Grants power upgrades; provides underwater breathing and regeneration; supportive stance
Persistent combat engagement; represents concentrated alien threat; culmination of harvest cycle

notes: Ecco communicates world-state primarily through environmental disruption (waterspout, storm spread) and NPC behavioral change. Safety and danger are signaled through ecosystem stability vs. chaos. The game uses temporal mechanics (past/present/alien world) as distinct visual-behavioral domains. Faction identity emerges from habitat association (ocean=friendly cetaceans; ruins=Atlantean tech; void=Vortex). The song mechanic serves dual narrative purpose: communication tool and power system, reinforcing Ecco's role as bridge between natural and ancient/alien knowledge. Glyphs function as both environmental obstacle and lore delivery system, encoding world history in interactive objects.
